source.title: Functional rare and low frequency variants in BLK and BANK1 contribute to human lupus

initial_request.research_problem: SLE is a heterogeneous autoimmune disease with a strong genetic component, but common GWAS variants explain only a modest fraction of risk. The study investigates whether rare coding variants in known SLE-risk genes contribute to disease, focusing on functional consequences in BLK and BANK1.
initial_request.target_object: Rare coding variants in BLK and BANK1
initial_request.known_context: GWAS have identified common variants associated with SLE, but they explain only a modest fraction of heritability. Rare coding variants with strong effects may account for missing heritability.
constraints: Focus on rare coding variants (MAF < 0.01) in 76 SLE-risk genes
Functional validation required for prioritized variants
input_data: WES/WGS data from SLE1 cohort (69 probands), SLE2 cohort (64 probands), and 97 healthy controls
Targeted PCR-amplifluor assay for BANK1 W40C in 150 SLE patients and 222 controls
quantifiable_goals: Identify rare coding variants in SLE-risk genes
Determine functional impact of BLK variants on kinase activity and IFNβ repression
Validate findings in CRISPR-edited B-cell lines and mouse models

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Common GWAS variants explain only a modest fraction of SLE heritability.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Missing heritability in SLE may be due to rare coding variants with strong effects.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Rare coding variants in lupus-risk genes contribute to SLE susceptibility.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Screen for rare coding variants in 76 SLE-risk genes using WES/WGS in SLE patients and controls.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Hypothesis formulated based on prior knowledge of missing heritability in complex diseases.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Need to design a study to test the hypothesis by screening for rare variants.

evidence.quote_or_span: Another hypothesis is that in some cases SLE arises from rare genetic variants with strong effects.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Frequency of rare BLK missense variants in SLE vs. other cohorts.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Rare BLK missense variants are more frequent in SLE patients than in other disease cohorts.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compare BLK variant frequencies in SLE1, SLE2, CVID/CID, and healthy controls.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Rare BLK missense variants found in 10.1% of SLE1 and 10.9% of SLE2 patients, significantly less in CVID/CID (2.8%) but comparable to healthy controls (7.2%).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: High frequency in controls suggests need for functional assays to distinguish pathogenic variants.

evidence.quote_or_span: 10.1% of our original SLE patients (SLE1, n = 7 of 69) (Fig. 2a) and 10.9% of our replication cohort (SLE2, n = 7 of 64) had a rare or novel missense SNV in BLK.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: BLK variants identified, but some also found in controls.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Do BLK variants interact with variants in other genes to cause disease?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Epistasis between BLK and BANK1 variants contributes to SLE, as suggested by co-segregation in families.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Investigate co-segregation of BLK and BANK1 variants in families with autoimmune disease.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Two families showed co-segregation of rare BLK SNVs with rare BANK1 mutations (D400G and W40C).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Search for BANK1 W40C in additional SLE cohort to assess frequency.

evidence.quote_or_span: we considered the possibility of additional SLE rare variants acting in epistasis with BLK being present in subjects with disease.

In search for variants in BLK- interacting proteins, we identified 2 families in which there was co-segregation in patients with autoimmune disease of a rare BLK SNV with a rare and a low-frequency mutation in B-Cell Scaffold Protein With Ankyrin Repeats 1 (BANK1) (BANK1 $^{D400G}$ rs201960198, ExAC MAF<0.00007 and BANK1 $^{W40C}$ rs35978636, ExAC MAF:0.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Hypothesis that BLK variants impair kinase activity.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Do BLK variants fail to phosphorylate BANK1?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Rare BLK variants have impaired ability to phosphorylate BANK1.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Co-express BLK variants and BANK1 in HEK293T cells and measure phosphorylation.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Four rare BLK variants showed impaired phosphorylation of BANK1.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Impaired BANK1 phosphorylation suggests IRF5 may be a shared substrate; hypothesize IRF5 as substrate.

evidence.quote_or_span: the four rare BLK variants tested had impaired phosphorylation of BANK1
upon co-expression of BLK and IRF5 in HEK293T cells, wild-type BLK phosphorylated IRF5, whereas the identified rare BLK variants had diminished or no ability to phosphorylate IRF5

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Do BLK variants impair IRF5 phosphorylation?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Rare BLK variants have diminished ability to phosphorylate IRF5.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Co-express BLK and IRF5 in HEK293T cells and assess phosphorylation.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Wild-type BLK phosphorylated IRF5; rare BLK variants had diminished or no ability to phosphorylate IRF5.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Functional impairment suggests need to compare patient vs. control variants in a relevant assay.

evidence.quote_or_span: upon co-expression of BLK and IRF5 in HEK293T cells, wild-type BLK phosphorylated IRF5, whereas the identified rare BLK variants had diminished or no ability to phosphorylate IRF5

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Patient BLK variants impair IRF5 phosphorylation, but some variants also found in controls.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Are patient BLK variants more damaging than control variants in repressing IFNβ?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Rare BLK variants from SLE patients impair IFNβ repression, while control variants do not.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Generate plasmids for six rare BLK variants found exclusively in healthy controls.

parameters.variants: six rare BLK variants exclusively found in healthy controls

evolution_trajectory.observation: Plasmids expressing each of the six rare BLK variants exclusively found in healthy controls were generated.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Compare IFNβ repression side-by-side with patient variants.

evidence.quote_or_span: we generated plasmids expressing each of the six rare BLK variants exclusively found in healthy controls

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Plasmids for six control BLK variants generated.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Do patient BLK variants impair IFNβ repression more than control variants?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Patient BLK variants impair IFNβ repression, while control variants do not.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Test side-by-side the repressive ability of all BLK variants using IFNβ dual luciferase reporter assay.

parameters.variants_tested: six control variants, multiple patient variants

evolution_trajectory.observation: Patient variants impaired IFNβ repression; none of the six control variants showed similar impairment (p<0.0001).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validate IFNβ repression impairment in a more detailed luciferase assay with additional controls.

evidence.quote_or_span: tested side-by-side the repressive ability of all BLK variants
none of the six rare BLK variants found exclusively in healthy controls had a similar impairment (p<0.0001)

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Patient BLK variants impair IFNβ repression in side-by-side comparison.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to confirm in a standardized IFNβ luciferase assay with IRF5 and MyD88 co-expression.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Patient BLK variants fail to repress IRF5-mediated IFNβ activity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Co-transfect HEK293T cells with IRF5, MyD88, and BLK variants, and measure IFNβ luciferase activity.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Patient BLK variants (R131W, R238Q, Y350H) failed to repress IFNβ activity compared to wild-type BLK (p<0.05, p<0.01, p<0.001).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validate findings in a more physiologically relevant B-cell line using CRISPR-Cas9.

evidence.quote_or_span: IFNβ luciferase activity 24 h after co-transfection of HEK293T cells with IRF5, MyD88, and indicated BLK variants.

Expression of the BLK variants with an IFNb dual luciferase reporter demonstrated that all tested BLK variants ( $BLK^{R131W}$ , $BLK^{R238Q}$ , and $BLK^{Y350H}$ ) were unable to repress IRF5-mediated IFNb activity compared to wild-type BLK (Fig.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Patient BLK variants impair IFNβ repression in HEK293T overexpression system.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Does the BLK R131W variant impair IFNβ repression in a human B-cell line?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: CRISPR-Cas9 edited Ramos cells homozygous for R131W will show impaired IFNβ repression upon TLR7/8 stimulation.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Generate CRISPR-Cas9 edited Ramos cells homozygous for BLK R131W and measure IFNβ luciferase after R848 stimulation.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Ramos cells with R131W showed impaired repression of IFNβ after R848 stimulation (p<0.05, p<0.01).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Functional validation of BANK1 variant interaction with TRAF6.

evidence.quote_or_span: IFNβ luciferase expression in CRISPR-Cas9 edited Ramos cells homozygous for the R131W variant, 24 h after stimulation with resiquimod (R848).
IFNβ luciferase activity 24 h after co-transfection of HEK293T cells with IRF5, MyD88, and indicated BLK variants.
upon co-expression of BLK and IRF5 in HEK293T cells, wild-type BLK phosphorylated IRF5, whereas the identified rare BLK variants had diminished or no ability to phosphorylate IRF5

As expected, upon transfection of $BLK^{R131W}$ , $BLK^{R131Q}$ and $BLK^{R238Q}$ into HEK293T cells almost no active bands were seen, compared with visible active bands in cells transfected with wild type BLK, $BLK^{Y305H}$ and a constitutively active $BLK^{Y501F}$ (Fig.
We confirmed that BANK1 formed a complex with TRAF6 by coimmunoprecipitation upon expression in HEK293T cells
Quantification of the percentage of HEK293T cells expressing WT or W40C BANK1-V5 with cytoplasmic inclusion bodies.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: BANK1 W40C identified, but mechanism unknown.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Does BANK1 interact with TRAF6, and does W40C alter this interaction?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: BANK1 forms a complex with TRAF6, and W40C may affect this interaction.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Confirm BANK1-TRAF6 interaction by coimmunoprecipitation in HEK293T cells.
evolution_trajectory.observation: BANK1 formed a complex with TRAF6, confirmed by two-way coimmunoprecipitation.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Investigate if W40C affects BANK1 subcellular localization and inclusion body formation.

evidence.quote_or_span: We confirmed that BANK1 formed a complex with TRAF6 by coimmunoprecipitation upon expression in HEK293T cells
Coimmunoprecipitation of BANK1-V5 and FLAG-TRAF6 from HEK293T cells demonstrating a two-way interaction.

Indeed, when we searched for this variant in a third cohort comprised of 150 SLE patients (for which the DNA yield and quality was insufficient for WES, but adequate for a targeted polymerase chain reaction (PCR)-amplifluor assay), we identified the $BANK1^{W40C}$ SNV in 9 SLE probands (6%) and in 3 out of 222 unaffected controls (n = 3 of 222, 1.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: BANK1 interacts with TRAF6; W40C may alter localization.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Does BANK1 W40C affect inclusion body formation?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: BANK1 W40C impairs inclusion body formation compared to wild-type.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform immunofluorescence staining of HEK293T cells expressing wild-type or W40C BANK1-V5 and quantify inclusion bodies.

parameters.quantification: double-blind counting of at least 100 cells per experiment, three replicates

evolution_trajectory.observation: 58% of cells with WT BANK1 had inclusions vs. 22% with W40C.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Assess functional consequence on IRF5 nuclear localization.

evidence.quote_or_span: Indirect immunofluorescence staining of HEK293T cells expressing either wild type (top panels) or W40C (bottom panels) BANK1-V5 cDNA constructs.
Quantification of the percentage of HEK293T cells expressing WT or W40C BANK1-V5 with cytoplasmic inclusion bodies.
Double blind cell counting was carried out on at least 100 cells per experiment with three experimental replicates.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: BANK1 W40C reduces inclusion bodies; TRAF6 and IRF5 are downstream.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Does BANK1 W40C affect IRF5 nuclear localization?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: BANK1 W40C impairs TRAF6-mediated IRF5 nuclear translocation.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Co-express IRF5, TRAF6, and BANK1 variants in HEK293T cells and measure nuclear IRF5 by immunofluorescence.

parameters.expressed_proteins: IRF5, FLAG-TRAF6, WT or W40C BANK1-V5

evolution_trajectory.observation: W40C BANK1 resulted in increased nuclear IRF5 compared to WT BANK1 (p<0.05, p<0.01).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Findings support a loss-of-function mechanism for BANK1 W40C promoting type I IFN.

evidence.quote_or_span: Representative images of indirect immunofluorescence staining of HEK293T cells expressing IRF5, FLAG-TRAF6 and either WT BANK1-V5 (top panel) or W40C BANK1-V5 (bottom panel).

validation_methods: CRISPR-Cas9 edited Ramos B-cell line homozygous for BLK R131W
Immunofluorescence for BANK1 inclusion bodies and IRF5 nuclear localization
metrics: Percentage of cells with inclusion bodies
success_verification.final_conclusion: Rare coding variants in BLK and BANK1 contribute to SLE by impairing kinase activity and IFNβ repression, with epistatic interactions. BLK variants from patients, but not controls, fail to repress IFNβ. BANK1 W40C is a loss-of-function variant that promotes type I IFN signaling by altering TRAF6 localization and IRF5 activation.
limitations: Mouse model with Blk R125W did not show increased type I IFN response, possibly due to species differences or environmental factors.
Incomplete penetrance of autoimmunity in heterozygous individuals suggests additional genetic or environmental factors.
Functional assays primarily in overexpression systems (HEK293T) may not fully recapitulate endogenous B-cell context.

risk_notes: Mouse model phenotype not included as a step due to negative result, but noted in limitations.
Some evidence IDs from auto_specific were used to supplement quotes, but all are from the same paper.
